# YUMMY — User Stories
AI-Native SDLC Platform (Platform-First, IDE-Agnostic)

Version: 1.1.0
Last Updated: 2026-04-16
Total User Stories: 500

## Table of Contents
- Epic 1: Platform Foundation and Onboarding (US-001 – US-025)
- Epic 2: Configuration and Provider Orchestration (US-026 – US-050)
- Epic 3: Repository and Knowledge Base Intelligence (US-051 – US-075)

- Epic 5: Conversational Engineering and RAG Experience (US-101 – US-125)

- Epic 7: Backlog Planning and Delivery Coordination (US-151 – US-175)
- Epic 8: Architecture Graph Wiki and Insights (US-176 – US-200)
- Epic 9: Quality Security Observability and Governance Core (US-201 – US-225)
- Epic 10: Extensibility Plugins and Ecosystem Expansion (US-226 – US-250)
- Epic 11: Business Analyst Experience (US-251 – US-275)
- Epic 12: System Analyst Experience (US-276 – US-300)
- Epic 13: Solution Architect Experience (US-301 – US-325)
- Epic 14: Product Owner Experience (US-326 – US-350)
- Epic 15: Project Manager Experience (US-351 – US-375)
- Epic 16: Developer Experience (US-376 – US-400)

- Epic 20: Governance and Compliance Experience (US-476 – US-500)

## Section A — Platform Stories (Heart of the System)
These stories define reusable platform capabilities. Role stories consume these capabilities instead of duplicating logic.

## Epic 1: Platform Foundation and Onboarding
Story Range: US-001 – US-025

Epic Objective:
- Deliver a reusable platform capability for platform onboarding and workspace initialization with consistent UX across tools.

### US-001: Guided Intake Experience
As a platform admin
I want a guided intake flow for platform onboarding and workspace initialization
So that I can capture complete inputs in one pass

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/components/workspace/OnboardingWizard.tsx`
- `frontend/components/workspace/SettingsPanel.tsx`
- `backend/routers/config_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Platform Foundation and Onboarding — US-001

  Scenario: Complete guided intake experience flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from guided intake experience failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

So that I can reduce setup time and avoid blank-page friction

- `frontend/components/workspace/SettingsPanel.tsx`

- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.

- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Configuration and Provider Orchestration — US-027

  Scenario: Complete smart template starter flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from smart template starter failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-041: Mobile-Responsive Journey
As a platform operator
I want mobile-responsive screens for AI provider configuration and switching
So that I can review and approve work on any device

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- `frontend/components/workspace/SettingsPanel.tsx`
- `backend/routers/config_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Configuration and Provider Orchestration — US-041

  Scenario: Complete mobile-responsive journey flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from mobile-responsive journey failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

## Epic 3: Repository and Knowledge Base Intelligence

Epic Objective:
- Deliver a reusable platform capability for repository ingestion and knowledge indexing with consistent UX across tools.

- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.

  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.

  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Workspace Sessions and Collaboration — US-095

  Scenario: Complete sla and deadline signaling flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from sla and deadline signaling failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

So that I can ensure consistent behavior across tools

Codebase Context:
- `backend/routers/ask_router.py`
- `frontend/components/workspace/ChatPanel.tsx`
- `frontend/components/workspace/RagPanel.tsx`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Conversational Engineering and RAG Experience — US-122

  Scenario: Complete ide-agnostic execution flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from ide-agnostic execution failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: SDLC Multi-Agent Orchestration — US-135

  Scenario: Complete personalized workspace flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from personalized workspace failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-149: Retry and Rollback
As a workflow owner
I want retry and rollback controls for multi-agent SDLC pipeline orchestration
So that I can safely recover from partial failures

Codebase Context:
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`
- `frontend/components/workspace/SdlcPanel.tsx`
- `frontend/components/workspace/BacklogPanel.tsx`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: SDLC Multi-Agent Orchestration — US-149

  Scenario: Complete retry and rollback flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from retry and rollback failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

## Epic 8: Architecture Graph Wiki and Insights
Story Range: US-176 – US-200

Epic Objective:
- Deliver a reusable platform capability for graph, wiki, and architecture insight exploration with consistent UX across tools.

### US-176: Guided Intake Experience
As a architecture analyst
I want a guided intake flow for graph, wiki, and architecture insight exploration
So that I can capture complete inputs in one pass

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/components/workspace/NodeGraph.tsx`
- `frontend/components/workspace/WikiPanel.tsx`
- `frontend/components/workspace/InsightsPanel.tsx`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Architecture Graph Wiki and Insights — US-176

  Scenario: Complete guided intake experience flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from guided intake experience failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-203: Contextual Recommendation Panel
As a platform governance lead
I want context-aware recommendations during quality gates, security controls, and observability
So that I can reduce ambiguity and rework

Codebase Context:
- `backend/routers/metrics_router.py`
- `frontend/components/workspace/TracingPanel.tsx`
- `backend/routers/config_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Quality Security Observability and Governance Core — US-203

  Scenario: Complete contextual recommendation panel flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Quality Security Observability and Governance Core — US-216

  Scenario: Complete mobile-responsive journey flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from mobile-responsive journey failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- `backend/main.py`
- `backend/models.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

### US-244: Role-Aware Permissioning
As a ecosystem architect
I want role-based controls in plugin onboarding and ecosystem expansion
So that I can protect sensitive actions and data

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/lib/api.ts`
- `backend/main.py`
- `backend/models.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

---

## Section B — Role-Specific Stories (Separated from Platform)
Each role-specific story inherits platform capabilities while focusing on role outcomes and UX quality.

## Epic 11: Business Analyst Experience
Story Range: US-251 – US-275

Epic Objective:
- Optimize the business analyst journey for business case and requirements refinement with minimal friction and clear handoffs.

  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Business Analyst Experience — US-271

  Scenario: Complete integration webhooks flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from integration webhooks failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-285: Personalized Workspace

I want personalized defaults for system analysis and contract definition
So that I can work faster with fewer repetitive actions

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

### US-299: Retry and Rollback
As a system analyst
I want retry and rollback controls for system analysis and contract definition
So that I can safely recover from partial failures

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Solution Architect Experience — US-312

  Scenario: Complete notification controls flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from notification controls failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

## Epic 14: Product Owner Experience
Story Range: US-326 – US-350

Epic Objective:
- Optimize the product owner journey for product prioritization and outcome management with minimal friction and clear handoffs.

### US-326: Guided Intake Experience
As a product owner
I want a guided intake flow for product prioritization and outcome management
So that I can capture complete inputs in one pass

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Product Owner Experience — US-326

  Scenario: Complete guided intake experience flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from guided intake experience failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-340: Audit and Trace Trail
As a product owner
I want an auditable trail for product prioritization and outcome management
So that I can meet compliance and forensic needs

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Project Manager Experience — US-353

  Scenario: Complete contextual recommendation panel flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from contextual recommendation panel failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

I want full keyboard navigation in delivery planning and risk control
So that I can improve speed and accessibility

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Project Manager Experience — US-367

  Scenario: Complete keyboard-only navigation flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from keyboard-only navigation failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-381: Automated Artifact Generation

So that I can create downstream outputs with less manual effort

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

### US-408: Dependency and Risk Alerts
As a operations engineer
I want proactive risk alerts in deployment reliability and production operations
So that I can address blockers before delays happen

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Operations and SRE Experience — US-408

  Scenario: Complete dependency and risk alerts flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from dependency and risk alerts failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

  Scenario: Complete integration webhooks flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from integration webhooks failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-422: IDE-Agnostic Execution
As a operations engineer
I want IDE/CLI-agnostic flows for deployment reliability and production operations
So that I can ensure consistent behavior across tools

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Operations and SRE Experience — US-422

  Scenario: Complete ide-agnostic execution flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Security Experience — US-435

  Scenario: Complete personalized workspace flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from personalized workspace failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Security Experience — US-449

  Scenario: Complete retry and rollback flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from retry and rollback failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

### US-463: Bulk Operations
As a IT service desk admin
I want bulk actions for incident triage and support handoffs
So that I can complete repetitive work efficiently

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

## Epic 20: Governance and Compliance Experience
Story Range: US-476 – US-500

Epic Objective:
- Optimize the governance lead journey for control monitoring and audit readiness with minimal friction and clear handoffs.

### US-476: Guided Intake Experience
As a governance lead
I want a guided intake flow for control monitoring and audit readiness
So that I can capture complete inputs in one pass

- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`

### US-490: Audit and Trace Trail
As a governance lead
I want an auditable trail for control monitoring and audit readiness
So that I can meet compliance and forensic needs

Codebase Context:
- `frontend/app/workspace/[sessionId]/page.tsx`
- `frontend/lib/types.ts`
- `backend/routers/sdlc_router.py`

Implementation Breakdown:
- Task 1: Finalize UX flow and interaction details.
  - Subtask 1.1: Define exact screen states (empty, loading, success, failure) and transition rules.
  - Subtask 1.2: Finalize copy, helper text, button labels, and inline validation messages.
- Task 2: Implement frontend behavior in Next.js.
  - Subtask 2.1: Build/extend React components, state transitions, and API hooks for this story.
  - Subtask 2.2: Add keyboard navigation, ARIA labels, focus order, and responsive layout behavior.
- Task 3: Implement backend contract and orchestration.
  - Subtask 3.1: Add or extend endpoint/service contract with strict request-response schema validation.
  - Subtask 3.2: Emit domain events with correlation IDs and retry-safe idempotency handling.
- Task 4: Integrate deterministic quality and policy controls.
  - Subtask 4.1: Add validation rules, permission checks, and guardrails for prohibited actions.
  - Subtask 4.2: Add structured error mapping so UI receives consistent recoverable/nonrecoverable states.
- Task 5: Verify end-to-end behavior and observability.
  - Subtask 5.1: Add unit, integration, and E2E tests mapped directly to acceptance scenarios.
  - Subtask 5.2: Emit telemetry (latency, success rate, abandonment, error type) to tracing dashboards.

Acceptance Criteria:

Feature: Governance and Compliance Experience — US-490

  Scenario: Complete audit and trace trail flow
    Given the user has required permissions and a valid active workspace
    When the user completes the workflow from the UI
    Then the system persists a valid result and displays success with next-step actions
    And an auditable event with correlation ID is recorded

  Scenario: Recover from audit and trace trail failure
    Given the workflow is in progress
    When a recoverable backend or validation error occurs
    Then the user sees a precise error message with recovery options
    And no partial/corrupt state is committed

---

## Definition of Ready and Done (applies to all 500 stories)
- Every story has mapped UX metrics: completion time, error rate, abandonment, and user satisfaction.
- Every story has deterministic validation and policy checks where applicable.
- Every story has traceability: business case -> story -> architecture/design -> implementation -> tests -> deployment evidence.
- Every story has role-appropriate permissions and audit logging.
- Every story has complete test coverage for success path and failure recovery path.
